@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python (call_K1BKO2Dgj8fhnle2E1JuCH5d), the agent counted only months that had cases (ignoring zero-count months within the past 10-month window) and applied a > mean + 2*std threshold, resulting in no "significant" month and returning None, whereas including zero months would have correctly identified November as the standout.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving OrderItem IDs for the product and filtering Cases within the last 10 months (calls call_HVKXHgc8YHtxtMpBaGyYIqTZ and call_TTZmBWCXZqo2RFqzxTU1YguC), the agent’s Python step (call_EE1MhUIudOo6h9OVAc9Sgy5l) applied an overly strict “significance” rule (top_count > mean + 2*std) computed only over months with at least one case, yielding null and leading to the final answer "None" instead of the true peak month, November.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

The agent applied an arbitrary significance test (max > mean + 2*std) in execute_python on the filtered cases and returned "No month," even though the grouped counts clearly peak in November, so adding this threshold (not requested by the query) led to the wrong answer.
